q: What trait do you judge hardest in others?
options: Selfishness
Weakness
Dishonesty
Neediness

q: When you're alone and no one's watching:
options: I finally let myself be lazy
I cry more than people think
I'm meaner in my thoughts than I'd admit
I need people more than I show

q: The compliment that makes you uncomfortable:
options: 'You deserve to rest'
'It's okay to not be strong'
'You don't have to be nice all the time'
'You're allowed to ask for help'
Dropping everything and being irresponsible
Breaking down in front of someone
Telling everyone what I really think
Being completely taken care of

q: The version of you that scares you:
options: The one who doesn't care about anyone
The one who falls apart completely
The one who says the cruel quiet part out loud
The one who can't function alone

q: You were taught as a child that:
options: Being selfish is the worst thing
Crying is weakness
You must always be kind and agreeable
You shouldn't need anyone

q: The emotion you suppress the most:
options: Anger — I swallow it
Sadness — I perform being okay
Resentment — I smile through it
Loneliness — I'd never admit it

q: People would be shocked to learn:
options: How little I actually want to give sometimes
How fragile I feel inside
How much I curate what I show
How afraid I am of being alone
Everyone leaves because I took too much
I collapse and no one catches me
My mask falls off and everyone sees
I call out and no one comes

q: If your shadow could speak, it would say:
options: 'Let me want things without guilt'
'Let me be soft without shame'
'Let me be honest without punishment'
'Let me need someone without losing myself'
